title: Iron on a vegetarian diet
text: Plant foods provide non-heme iron, which the body absorbs less efficiently than the heme iron found in meat, roughly 2 to 20 percent versus 15 to 35 percent. Good vegetarian sources include legumes such as lentils and beans, tofu, dark leafy greens, seeds, nuts, wholegrains and fortified cereals; eggs also contain non-heme iron. Because absorption is lower, vegetarians benefit from pairing iron-rich foods with a source of vitamin C, and from keeping tea, coffee and high-calcium foods to a different time than their main iron meal.
